company_name: Compagnie Générale des Établissements Michelin Société en commandite par actions
description: Compagnie Générale des Établissements Michelin Société en commandite par actions manufactures and sells tires worldwide. The company offers tires for private use covering cars, racing, biking, motorcycles, scooters, and mopeds; and professional use, such as freight and people transport, agriculture, construction and industrial, mining and quarries, corporate fleets, tradesmen and professionals, civil and military operations, light rail, and aircraft. It is also involved in the provision of tire-related services, including development of mobility solutions for fleet managers, vehicle manufacturers, farmers, distributors, and individuals; mobility services, such as road maps, mobile apps, itineraries, and travel guides; lifestyle products comprising car and bike accessories, shoe soles, and sports and leisure gears; and high-tech materials that include 3D metal printing, specialty, rubber, biosourced, and recycled materials. Compagnie Générale des Établissements Michelin Société en commandite par actions was incorporated in 1863 and is based in Clermont-Ferrand, France.
ai_description: Compagnie Générale des Établissements Michelin SCA (Michelin) manufactures and distributes tires for passenger cars and light trucks, trucks and buses, aircraft, motorcycles, bicycles, agricultural and construction equipment, mining and off-the-road vehicles; it also provides mobility services, lifestyle products including travel guides and maps, Michelin engineering and services, polymer composite solutions such as coated fabrics, technical films, high-tech seals and metal 3D printing, as well as fleet solutions leveraging smart objects and data analytics. The company operates through three main segments: Automotive and Two-wheel tires and related distribution, Road Transportation tires and related distribution, and Specialty Businesses including mining, off-the-road, aircraft and high-tech materials; it maintains 123 production sites in 26 countries, a commercial presence in 170 countries, and targets original equipment manufacturers, replacement markets, fleets and specialty sectors worldwide with significant operations in Europe, North America, Asia-Pacific and other regions. Founded in 1889 and headquartered in Clermont-Ferrand, France, Michelin owns brands including Kleber, BFGoodrich, Uniroyal and SASCAR, and employs around 125,000 people globally. In recent developments, the company strengthened its high-margin mining and aircraft tire segments amid a 2025 strategy prioritizing resource reallocation for margin expansion, launched new products such as the MICHELIN CrossClimate3 Sport tire and renewals of the Primacy and CrossClimate ranges, advanced its All-Sustainable strategy through 2030 with expansion in connected services and polymer composites, acquired Tyroola in December 2023 and formed a partnership with Genesis Motor America in July 2024, while implementing industrial restructuring in Poland, China, Sri Lanka and France to enhance competitiveness following a record 2024 with €3.4 billion segment operating income.
